hook.text: When a fire alarm rings at work, most people finish their email before standing up.

buildup.text: Normalcy bias makes us assume that things will keep functioning the way they always have.

discovery.text: It explains why people stay home during hurricane warnings or ignore tsunami sirens.

twist.text: Up to 70% of people in disasters initially do nothing—waiting for someone to confirm it's real.

climax.text: Overcoming normalcy bias requires accepting that rare events do happen—and to you.

punchline.text: It can't happen here—until it does.

quiz.question: What is normalcy bias?
options: Assuming things will keep functioning normally despite warning signs
